title: rhizophora × selala
intro: Rhizophora × selala, also known as the mangrove apple, is a plant species that is a hybrid of Rhizophora mangle and Bruguiera gymnorrhiza, commonly found in tropical coastal ecosystems. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, including antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which contribute to its health benefits. The health benefits of Rhizophora × selala include reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and exhibiting antioxidant activity. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as teas and infusions, which are consumed to alleviate various health issues.
